attributes.name: Title
attributes.value: MitoEM 2.0 - A Benchmark for Challenging 3D Mitochondria Instance Segmentation from EM Images

attributes.name: Title
attributes.value: MitoEM 2.0 - A Benchmark for Challenging 3D Mitochondria Instance Segmentation from EM Images

attributes.name: Description
attributes.value: We present MitoEM 2.0, a curated benchmark resource for training and evaluating three-dimensional (3D) mitochondria instance segmentation in volume electron microscopy (vEM). The collection assembles multiscale vEM datasets (FIB-SEM, SBF-SEM, and ssSEM) spanning diverse tissues and species, with expert-verified instance labels emphasizing biologically difficult scenarios, including dense mitochondrial packing, hyperfused networks, and thin filamentous connections with ambiguous boundaries.  All releases include native-resolution volumes and standardized processed versions, per-volume metadata (voxel size, modality, tissue, and data splits), and official train/validation/test partitions to enable reproducible benchmarking. Annotations follow a consistent protocol with quality checks and instance reindexing. Data are provided in NIfTI format with an nnU-Net–compatible layout, alongside machine-readable split files and checksums. MitoEM 2.0 facilitates robust model development and fair comparison across methods while supporting reuse in bioimage analysis, algorithm benchmarking, and teaching.

attributes.name: Sample preparation protocol
attributes.value: High-pressure freezing followed by freeze substitution

attributes.name: Title
attributes.value: Serial section alignment for volume EM

attributes.name: Spatial and temporal alignment
attributes.value: Spatial alignment was performed across serial sections to reconstruct three-dimensional volumes. No temporal alignment was involved, as all datasets represent static biological specimens acquired using volume electron microscopy.

attributes.name: Title
attributes.value: Mitochondria instance segmentation and quality control

attributes.name: Image analysis overview
attributes.value: Image analysis consisted of semi-automatic mitochondria instance segmentation followed by extensive manual proofreading and quality control. Initial segmentation was generated using automated or learning-based tools to produce candidate mitochondrial instances. Expert annotators then manually refined the segmentations to correct merge and split errors, ensure biologically meaningful instance separation, and resolve ambiguous thin connections.  All annotations were reviewed for topological consistency and reindexed to ensure unique instance identifiers. Final instance masks were validated by domain experts prior to release and organized in a standardized format to support reproducible benchmarking and downstream method development.

attributes.name: Title
attributes.value: Instance-level mitochondria annotations for volume EM

attributes.name: Annotation Overview
attributes.value: This dataset provides instance-level mitochondria segmentation annotations for 3D volume electron microscopy (vEM) datasets. Each mitochondrion is annotated as a unique instance, including biologically challenging cases such as densely packed mitochondria, hyperfused networks, and thin filamentous connections with ambiguous boundaries.  Annotations were generated using a semi-automatic workflow combining automated segmentation tools with extensive manual proofreading by expert annotators. Final instance masks were reviewed for biological plausibility, topological consistency, and labeling accuracy prior to release.

attributes.name: Annotation Method
attributes.value: Semi-automatic annotation. Initial mitochondria segmentation was generated using automated or learning-based algorithms to produce candidate instances. Expert annotators manually refined the results to correct merge and split errors, resolve ambiguous thin connections, and ensure biologically meaningful instance separation.

attributes.name: Description
attributes.value: 3D electron microscopy volumes of mouse pancreatic beta cells with instance-level mitochondria segmentation annotations, acquired using focused ion beam scanning electron microscopy (FIB-SEM) and fully proofread by domain experts.
